author: y4rr
content:
Phase 1B (#985) value-parity attempt failed at strict loader's UnmappedFixtureKey check.

Verbatim panic: 'FCN-ResNet50: loader failed on well-formed fixture: invalid argument: FCN-ResNet50 value-parity loader: torchvision key "classifier.1.bias" has no mapping to a ferrotorch parameter and is not a declared BN buffer'

Root cause: torchvision fcn_resnet50 wraps a ResNet50 backbone with replace_stride_with_dilation=[False,True,True], producing dilated layer3/4 (stride=1, dilation=2/4 instead of stride=2). torchvision's state_dict prefixes the segmentation classifier as 'classifier.0..4' (the FCNHead Sequential). Ferrotorch's Fcn struct uses 'head.0/1/4' prefix — closer to torchvision but the key 'classifier.1.bias' (BN bias inside the head) suggests:
1. torchvision uses 'classifier.*' (top-level Sequential), ferrotorch uses 'head.*'.
2. Even if naming were aligned, ferrotorch's resnet50() backbone has standard strides (no dilation) so layer3/4 spatial sizes won't match the H/8 H/8 fcn classification expects.

Two structural divergences: (a) head prefix naming, (b) backbone dilation. Both prevent loader mapping.

Phase 1B test infra in place; tests #[ignore = "#994 ..."] until ferrotorch's resnet50 supports dilation and Fcn rekeys to torchvision's 'classifier.*' top-level prefix.
